zh.lifeSummary: 當Geoffrey de Mandeville 1st Earl of Essex於1098年出生在英國英格蘭艾可普萊時，他的父親William de Mandeville Constable at the Tower in London36歲，母親Margaret Rie22歲。他和Rohese de Vere至少有4個兒子和2個女兒。他於1140年被列為custodian/constable of the tower of london。他去世於1144年9月14日在英國素福爾可米爾登哈爾享年46歲，埋葬在英國素福爾可。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Geoffrey de Mandeville 1st Earl of Essex于1098年出生在英国英格兰艾可普萊时，他的父亲William de Mandeville Constable at the Tower in London36岁，母亲Margaret Rie22岁。他和Rohese de Vere至少有4个儿子和2个女儿。他于1140年被列为custodian/constable of the tower of london。他去世于1144年9月14日在英国素福爾可米爾登哈爾享年46岁，埋葬在英国素福爾可。

nameMeanings.name: Mandeville
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel, Andre, Armand, Gabrielle, Camille, Emile, Fernand, Fernande, Gaston, Jean-Paul, Lucien, Michel.

French: habitational name from Mandeville, the name of two places in Normandy, derived from Latin magna villa ‘large estate’, or a variant of Manneville, a habitational name from Manneville, the name of several places in Normandy (see 2 below). Compare Montville and Monville .
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Manneville (Seine-Maritime, Calvados), Manneville-sur-Risle (Eure), or Magneville (Manche).

variants.name: Mansfield
meanings: English: habitational name from a place so named in Nottinghamshire. The placename means ‘open land by the river Maun’, from the river name Maun (which may take its name from a hill named with Old English mamme ‘teat’) + Old English feld ‘open country’.
Irish (Waterford): when not an importation of 1, this is an altered form of the Norman name de Manville (see Mandeville 3).
Americanized form of German and Jewish (Ashkenazic) Mansfeld, a habitational name from a place so called in Saxony.

variants.name: Manderville
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Mandeville  .

variants.name: Manwell
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Mandeville  .

variants.name: Manville
meanings: English (Sussex and Lancashire): variant of   Mandeville  .
Altered form of   Mainville   1, a surname of Swiss French origin (see   Miville  ).
French: variant of Menville, a habitational name from Menville in Haute-Garonne, and also of Mainville 2.

variants.name: Mundell
meanings: Scottish (of Norman origin): variant of   Mandeville  .
Americanized form of German Mündel (or some other similar, like-sounding surname): nickname for someone with a prominent mouth, from Middle High German munt, mündelīn ‘(little) mouth’.

variants.name: Monville
meanings: Altered form of French   Mandeville  .
Walloon: habitational name from Monville in Namur province in Wallonia (Belgium), of the same etymology as in 3 below.
In some cases possibly also French: habitational name from Montville in Seine-Maritime or Monville in Calvados, both named with Old French mont ‘mountain, hill’ + ville ‘settlement’.

variants.name: Mandal
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Chandi, Sanjay, Sankar, Somnath, Aditi, Aditya, Ajit, Amala, Amitabha, Anil, Anirudh, Aparna.
Indian (northern states): from Sanskrit maṇḍala, a word that has several meanings, including ‘sphere’ and ‘halo’.
English (Cumbria; of Norman origin): variant of   Mandeville  .

variants.name: Montville
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Emile, Vernice.
Altered form of French   Mandeville  .
French: variant of   Monville  . This surname is very rare in France, found mainly in Creuse.

variants.name: Manfull
meanings: English (Midlands): probably a variant of   Mandeville  . Compare   Manville  .

nameMeanings.name: Geoffrey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of Germanic (Frankish and Lombard) origin, introduced to Britain by the Normans. It was in regular use among the counts of Anjou, ancestors of the English royal house of Plantagenet, who were descended from Geoffrey Plantagenet, Count of Anjou ( 1113–51 ). It was a particularly popular name in England and France in the later Middle Ages; notable bearers in England include the poet Geoffrey Chaucer ( c.1340–1400 ) and in Wales the chronicler Geoffrey of Monmouth ( d. 1155 ). The original form and meaning of the name is disputed. According to one theory, it is merely a variant of Godfrey ; others derive the first part from the Germanic word gawia ‘territory’, walah ‘stranger’, or gisil ‘pledge’. Medieval forms can be found to support all these theories, and it is possible that several names have fallen together, or that the name was subjected to reanalysis by folk etymology at an early date.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: When Geoffrey de Mandeville 1st Earl of Essex was born in 1098, in Pleshey, Essex, England, United Kingdom, his father, William de Mandeville Constable at the Tower in London, was 36 and his mother, Margaret Rie, was 22. He had at least 4 sons and 2 daughters with Rohese de Vere. In 1140, at the age of 44, his occupation is listed as custodian/constable of the tower of london. He died on 14 September 1144, in Mildenhall, Suffolk, England, at the age of 46, and was buried in Suffolk, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Geoffrey de Mandeville 1st Earl of Essex est né en 1098, à Pleshey, Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, William de Mandeville Constable at the Tower in London, avait 36 ans et sa mère, Margaret Rie, avait 22 ans. Il a eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles avec Rohese de Vere. En 1140, à l’âge de 44 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est custodian/constable of the tower of london. Il est décédé le 14 septembre 1144, à Mildenhall, Suffolk, Angleterre, à l’âge de 46 ans, et a été enterré à Suffolk, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Geoffrey de Mandeville 1st Earl of Essex 1098 in Pleshey, Essex, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater William de Mandeville Constable at the Tower in London 36 und seine Mutter Margaret Rie 22. Er hatte mit Rohese de Vere mindestens 4 Söhne und 2 Töchter. 1140, als er 44 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als custodian/constable of the tower of london aufgeführt. Er starb am 14. September 1144 im Alter von 46 Jahren in Mildenhall, Suffolk, England und wurde in Suffolk, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Geoffrey de Mandeville 1st Earl of Essex è nato il nel 1098 nel Pleshey, Essex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, William de Mandeville Constable at the Tower in London, aveva 36 anni e il suo madre, Margaret Rie, aveva 22 anni. Lui avuto almeno 4 figli e 2 figlie con Rohese de Vere. Nel 1140, all'età di 44 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come custodian/constable of the tower of london. Morì il 14 settembre 1144, nel Mildenhall, Suffolk, England, all'età di 46 anni, e fu sepolto a Suffolk, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Geoffrey de Mandeville 1st Earl of Essexが1098年にイギリスイギリスエセックスプレシーで生まれた時，父William de Mandeville Constable at the Tower in Londonは36歳，母Margaret Rieは22歳であった。彼はRohese de Vereとの間に少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘をもうけた。1140年，44歳の時，職業はcustodian/constable of the tower of londonと記録されている。彼は1144年9月14日にイギリススフォルクミルデンハルで46歳で死去し，イギリススフォルクにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Geoffrey de Mandeville 1st Earl of Essex(이)가 1098년 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 플레셰이에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William de Mandeville Constable at the Tower in London(은)는 36세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret Rie(은)는 22세였다. 그는 Rohese de Vere(와)과 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 1140년, 44세의 나이로 그의 직업은 custodian/constable of the tower of london이었다. 그는 1144년 9월 14일 영국 수폴크 미데홀에서 46세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 수폴크에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Geoffrey de Mandeville 1st Earl of Essex nasceu em 1098, em Pleshey, Essex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, William de Mandeville Constable at the Tower in London, tinha 36 anos e sua mãe, Margaret Rie, tinha 22 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 4 filhos e 2 filhas com Rohese de Vere. Em 1140, com 44 anos, sua profissão aparece como custodian/constable of the tower of london. Ele faleceu em 14 de setembro de 1144, em Mildenhall, Suffolk, Inglaterra, com 46 anos, e foi sepultado em Suffolk, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Geoffrey de Mandeville 1st Earl of Essex родился в 1098 г. в местности плеши, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени William de Mandeville Constable at the Tower in London было 36 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Rie, 22. У него было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери от Rohese de Vere. В 1140 году, в возрасте 44 лет,  он работал по профессии custodian/constable of the tower of london. Он умер 14 сентября 1144 г. в местности маилденолл, суффолк, Англия в возрасте 46 лет и был похоронен в местности суффолк, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Geoffrey de Mandeville 1st Earl of Essex nació en 1098, en Pleshey, Essex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, William de Mandeville Constable at the Tower in London, tenía 36 años y su mamá, Margaret Rie, tenía 22 años. Tuvo por lo menos 4 hijos y 2 hijas con Rohese de Vere. En 1140, a la edad de 44 años, su ocupación registrada era custodian/constable of the tower of london. Murió el 14 de septiembre de 1144, en Mildenhall, Suffolk, Inglaterra, a la edad de 46 años, y fue sepultado en Suffolk, Inglaterra.
